title: Lumbar clinic review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.
assessment.rationale: Supports low-back-pain onset, character, radiation, function and prior-treatment history for lumbar pain clinic review; no decision is generated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15
source.population: Adults presenting with acute low back pain.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for acute adult low-back-pain telehealth documentation in Dubai; partial for chronic pain, in-person examination, and settings outside Dubai.

exact_location.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5–6 Virtual assessment and clinical history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

assessment.text: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.
assessment.rationale: Supports systemic, trauma and neurological warning context for lumbar pain clinic review; no decision is generated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15
source.population: Adults presenting with acute low back pain.

source.exclusions: Direct for acute adult low-back-pain telehealth documentation in Dubai; partial for chronic pain, in-person examination, and settings outside Dubai.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.

assessment.text: Defines urgent and specialist review for severe or night pain, infection, trauma, neurological deficit, urinary or faecal dysfunction, systemic disease, persistent symptoms, and selected imaging needs.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-led referral context for lumbar pain clinic review; no decision is generated.
